free_text: (p,n) Cross Section and Proton Optical-Model
Parameters in the 4 to 5.5 MeV Energy Region
Energy spread of the beam within the target due to
straggling and primary inhomogeneity about 100 KeV.
Natural element targets with thickness of about
1 mg/cm**2, evaporated onto a Ta backing thick enough
to stop the proton beam.
Boron trifluoride long counter.
Long counter calibrated with a mock fission source
having a strength known to about 5% accuracy.
Cross sections given are averages for several repeated
measurements.
No corrections for charged-particle emission made;
in this energy region between Q-value and Coulomb
barrier neutron emission is strongly favoured over
charged particle emission.
Assumed accuracy in data.
Neutrons emitted from the targets were measured with
an instrumental error within about 10%.
Proton current was monitored to about 2% accuracy.
Data received by McGowen from Albert, 1964.
Converted from data set B0093 and updated
at NNDC. Originally compiled by KaChaPaG and updated
by CaJaD.
COMMENT BY COMPILER: 63Cu(p,n) threshold is 4.22 MeV,
therefore value at 3.6 MeV mostly represents 65Cu(p,n)
cross section.
COMMENT BY COMPILER: 64Zn threshold is 8.07 MeV; 66Zn
threshold is 6.05 MeV; 68Zn threshold is 3.76 MeV;
therefore, value at 3.6 MeV mostly represents
67Zn(p,n) cross section.
COMMENT BY COMPILER: May be small contribution at 8.1
MeV from 77Se(p,np).
COMMENT BY COMPILER: May be small contribution at 8.1
MeV from 95,97Mo(p,np).
COMMENT BY COMPILER: May be small contribution at 8.1
MeV from 105Pd(p,np).
COMMENT BY COMPILER: May be small contribution at 8.1
MeV from 111Cd(p,np).
COMMENT BY COMPILER: May be small contribution at
8.1 MeV from 115,117Sn(p,np) and at 7.0 and 8.1
MeV from 119Sn.
